raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By “make this stronger,” do you primarily want (A) sentence- and paragraph-level editing to make the prose more concise and assertive, (B) higher-level reframing of the contribution and claims (thesis/gap, argument flow, and alignment of claims to the reported evidence), or (C) both?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require sharpening the thesis and contribution framing so the core message is clearer and more compelling.",
    "R1.3": "Tightening alignment between claims and the reported results/ablations helps make the piece feel stronger without overreaching.",
    "R1.6": "“Make this stronger” commonly entails punchier, clearer sentences and improved flow, making writing polish a load-bearing need.",
    "RX.1": "Any strengthening should transparently convey uncertainty and limits to avoid misleading confidence.",
    "RX.2": "Bolder language risks overclaiming, so guarding against exaggerated novelty or performance is critical.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay within the scope of revising the text and framing rather than altering experiments or data.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding language that could prompt unwarranted deployment decisions helps reduce downstream harms.",
    "RX.5": "Maintaining traceable links to the stated sections, benchmarks, and prior version preserves provenance of claims."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: By “make this stronger,” do you primarily want (A) sentence- and paragraph-level editing to make the prose more concise and assertive, (B) higher-level reframing of the contribution and claims (thesis/gap, argument flow, and alignment of claims to the reported evidence), or (C) both?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require sharpening the thesis and contribution framing so the core message is clearer and more compelling.
rationale.R1.3: Tightening alignment between claims and the reported results/ablations helps make the piece feel stronger without overreaching.
rationale.R1.6: “Make this stronger” commonly entails punchier, clearer sentences and improved flow, making writing polish a load-bearing need.
rationale.RX.1: Any strengthening should transparently convey uncertainty and limits to avoid misleading confidence.
rationale.RX.2: Bolder language risks overclaiming, so guarding against exaggerated novelty or performance is critical.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within the scope of revising the text and framing rather than altering experiments or data.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding language that could prompt unwarranted deployment decisions helps reduce downstream harms.
rationale.RX.5: Maintaining traceable links to the stated sections, benchmarks, and prior version preserves provenance of claims.
